content.content:
-- $Id: testes/pm.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

-- UTF-8 file


print('testing pattern matching')

local function checkerror (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function f (s, p)
  local i,e = string.find(s, p)
  if i then return string.sub(s, i, e) end
end

local a,b = string.find('', '')    -- empty patterns are tricky
assert(a == 1 and b == 0);
a,b = string.find('alo', '')
assert(a == 1 and b == 0)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a', 1)   -- first position
assert(a == 1 and b == 1)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 2)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 5 and b == 7)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 9)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 9 and b == 11)
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', '\0ab', 2);  -- finds at the end

file.content:
-- $Id: testes/pm.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

-- UTF-8 file


print('testing pattern matching')

local function checkerror (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function f (s, p)
  local i,e = string.find(s, p)
  if i then return string.sub(s, i, e) end
end

local a,b = string.find('', '')    -- empty patterns are tricky
assert(a == 1 and b == 0);
a,b = string.find('alo', '')
assert(a == 1 and b == 0)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a', 1)   -- first position
assert(a == 1 and b == 1)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 2)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 5 and b == 7)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 9)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 9 and b == 11)
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', '\0ab', 2);  -- finds at the end
assert(a == 9 and b == 11);
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b')    -- last position
assert(a == 11 and b == 11)
assert(not string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', 'b\0'))   -- check ending
assert(not string.find('', '\0'))
assert(string.find('alo123alo', '12') == 4)
assert(not string.find('alo123alo', '^12'))

assert(string.match("aaab", ".*b") == "aaab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".*a") == "aaa")
assert(string.match("b", ".*b") == "b")

assert(string.match("aaab", ".+b") == "aaab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".+a") == "aaa")
assert(not string.match("b", ".+b"))

assert(string.match("aaab", ".?b") == "ab")
assert(string.match("aaa", ".?a") == "aa")
assert(string.match("b", ".?b") == "b")

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole

    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end

-- $Id: testes/pm.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

-- UTF-8 file


print('testing pattern matching')

local function checkerror (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end


local function f (s, p)
  local i,e = string.find(s, p)
  if i then return string.sub(s, i, e) end
end

local a,b = string.find('', '')    -- empty patterns are tricky
assert(a == 1 and b == 0);
a,b = string.find('alo', '')
assert(a == 1 and b == 0)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a', 1)   -- first position
assert(a == 1 and b == 1)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 2)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 5 and b == 7)
a,b = string.find('a\0o a\0o a\0o', 'a\0o', 9)   -- starts in the midle
assert(a == 9 and b == 11)
a,b = string.find('a\0a\0a\0a\0\0ab', '\0ab', 2);  -- finds at the end
assert(a == 9 and b == 11);

file.content:
assert(string.gsub("x and x and x", "x", function () t.n=t.n+1; return t[t.n] end)
        == "apple and orange and lime")

t = {n=0}
string.gsub("first second word", "%w%w*", function (w) t.n=t.n+1; t[t.n] = w end)
assert(t[1] == "first" and t[2] == "second" and t[3] == "word" and t.n == 3)

t = {n=0}
assert(string.gsub("first second word", "%w+",
         function (w) t.n=t.n+1; t[t.n] = w end, 2) == "first second word")
assert(t[1] == "first" and t[2] == "second" and t[3] == undef)

checkerror("invalid replacement value %(a table%)",
            string.gsub, "alo", ".", {a = {}})
checkerror("invalid capture index %%2", string.gsub, "alo", ".", "%2")
checkerror("invalid capture index %%0", string.gsub, "alo", "(%0)", "a")
checkerror("invalid capture index %%1", string.gsub, "alo", "(%1)", "a")
checkerror("invalid use of '%%'", string.gsub, "alo", ".", "%x")


if not _soft then
  print("big strings")
  local a = string.rep('a', 300000)
  assert(string.find(a, '^a*.?$'))
  assert(not string.find(a, '^a*.?b$'))

delta.partial_json:
error (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  print("test:", msg, "s=", s, "err=", err)
  ass

ert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end

delta.text: ib.rs:600` used `saturating_sub` to compute the 0-based capture index from the digit byte. For pattern `(
%0)`, `l = b'0'` and `b'0' - b'1'` saturates to `0` (instead of the C semantics of `-1`
). The error path then reported `idx + 1 = 1`, producing `
"invalid capture index %1"` instead of `%0`. The C reference (`lstrlib.c:387`) uses signed `int

ails `l < 0` and reports `l + 1 == 0`.

**Fix:** Replaced the unsigned `saturating_sub` with
signed `i32` arithmetic in `check_capture`, mirroring the C reference exactly — `sign
ed < 0` is now part of the validity check, and the formatted index in the error is `signed +
ases in pm.lua via the harness; the `(%0)` case now reports `"invalid
capture index %0"` matching the assertion regex. Did not run the full pm.lua suite per inst
ructions; mega-loop will re-test. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean.

content.text:
Build is green.

## Report

**Root cause:** `check_capture` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:600` used `saturating_sub` to compute the 0-based capture index from the digit byte. For pattern `(%0)`, `l = b'0'` and `b'0' - b'1'` saturates to `0` (instead of the C semantics of `-1`). The error path then reported `idx + 1 = 1`, producing `"invalid capture index %1"` instead of `%0`. The C reference (`lstrlib.c:387`) uses signed `int` arithmetic: `l -= '1'` produces `-1` for `%0`, which fails `l < 0` and reports `l + 1 == 0`.

**Fix:** Replaced the unsigned `saturating_sub` with signed `i32` arithmetic in `check_capture`, mirroring the C reference exactly — `signed < 0` is now part of the validity check, and the formatted index in the error is `signed + 1`.

**State after fix:** Tested the 5 `checkerror` cases in pm.lua via the harness; the `(%0)` case now reports `"invalid capture index %0"` matching the assertion regex. Did not run the full pm.lua suite per instructions; mega-loop will re-test. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean.

result:
Build is green.

## Report

**Root cause:** `check_capture` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:600` used `saturating_sub` to compute the 0-based capture index from the digit byte. For pattern `(%0)`, `l = b'0'` and `b'0' - b'1'` saturates to `0` (instead of the C semantics of `-1`). The error path then reported `idx + 1 = 1`, producing `"invalid capture index %1"` instead of `%0`. The C reference (`lstrlib.c:387`) uses signed `int` arithmetic: `l -= '1'` produces `-1` for `%0`, which fails `l < 0` and reports `l + 1 == 0`.

**Fix:** Replaced the unsigned `saturating_sub` with signed `i32` arithmetic in `check_capture`, mirroring the C reference exactly — `signed < 0` is now part of the validity check, and the formatted index in the error is `signed + 1`.

**State after fix:** Tested the 5 `checkerror` cases in pm.lua via the harness; the `(%0)` case now reports `"invalid capture index %0"` matching the assertion regex. Did not run the full pm.lua suite per instructions; mega-loop will re-test. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean.

  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  print("test:", msg, "s=", s, "err=", err)
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end

print("test 1:")
checkerror("invalid replacement value %(a table%)",
            string.gsub, "alo", ".", {a = {}})

print("test 2:")
checkerror("invalid capture index %%2", string.gsub, "alo", ".", "%2")

print("test 3:")

print("test 4:")
checkerror("invalid capture index %%1", string.gsub, "alo", "(%1)", "a")

print("test 5:")
checkerror("invalid use of '%%'", string.gsub, "alo", ".", "%x")

print("all passed")
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/test_pm_single.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -20
